data.title: Meta Platforms Stock: 'Buy the Pullback,' Says Top Jefferies Analyst Ahead of Q4 Earnings
data.description:
Social media giant Meta Platforms (META) is scheduled to announce its fourth-quarter results on January 28. Ahead of the Q4 earnings, top Jefferies analyst Brent Thill reiterated a Buy rating on META stock with a price target of $910. The stock rose 6% on Thursday, as Jefferies called META a Top Pick, saying that the 18% pullback since the company's Q3 earnings report presents an attractive buying opportunity. The stock also benefited from a rally in the broader market.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Meanwhile, Wall Street expects Meta Platforms to report earnings per share (EPS) of $8.19, reflecting a 2.1% year-over-year growth. Revenue is estimated to rise about 20.6% to $58.35 billion.

Jefferies Analyst Is Bullish on META Stock

Thill believes that following the decline in Meta Platforms' shares since Q3 2025 earnings, the stock trades at an attractive valuation. The 5-star analyst contended that while the decline reflects worries related to margin woes, high capital spending, and artificial intelligence (AI) execution, it presents a significant upside if META addresses these challenges, which he believes is possible.

Notably, Thill listed five reasons for his optimism:

  1. The analyst highlighted that following the selloff since Q3 earnings, META shares are trading at a lucrative risk-reward: an "8-turn PE discount" compared to Alphabet (GOOGL).
  2. Thill sees limited downside to estimates from current levels, but stronger upside, thanks to strength in revenue and the expectations of the company's efficiency gains offsetting higher operating expenses.
  3. The top-rated analyst is also upbeat about Meta Platforms' "all-star AI hires" and expects them to deliver results this year.
  4. Thill expects AI to continue to drive META's core advertising flywheel in 2026.
  5. Optimism about new growth engines, with Thill expecting WhatsApp to grow from about $9 billion currently to $36 billion by Fiscal 2029. He also sees additional upside from Threads and Llama/AI.

Is META a Good Stock to Buy Now?

Ahead of Q4 earnings, Wall Street has a Strong Buy consensus rating on Meta Platforms stock based on 37 Buys, six Holds, and one Sell recommendation. The average META stock price target of $823.66 indicates 27.2% upside potential.
data.author: Sirisha Bhogaraju

data.description:
Nvidia (NVDA) shares have been largely range-bound in recent months. Over the past six months, the stock has risen roughly 3%, underperforming the broader market, while the S&P 500 (SPX) has gained close to 8% over the same period.

data.title: ISRG Earnings: Intuitive Surgical's Financial Results Beat Wall Street's Expectations
data.description:
Intuitive Surgical's stock is rising (ISRG) after the medical device company reported fourth quarter 2025 financial results that surpassed Wall Street's expectations.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The company, which specializes in robotic surgery technology, posted earnings per share (EPS) of $2.53, which was above analysts' consensus estimate of $2.26. Revenue in the period came in at $2.87 billion, exceeding the $2.75 billion forecast on Wall Street.

Management attributed the strong print largely to 17% growth in the company's da Vinci robotic surgery platform, which saw procedures rise in the quarter. The company said that it placed 532 da Vinci surgical systems in the quarter, up from 493 a year earlier, and growing its installed base to 11,106 systems, a 12% year-over-year increase.

ISRG's Global Expansion

Looking ahead, Intuitive Surgical guided for 2026 worldwide da Vinci procedure growth of 13% to 15%, compared to 18% in 2025. The company expects gross profit margins to be between 67% and 68% of revenue, which includes an estimated 1.2% impact from tariffs.

"Our fourth quarter results reflect continued global adoption of our minimally invasive robotic platforms," said CEO Gary Guthart in the company's earnings release. "The placement of 303 da Vinci 5 systems this quarter demonstrates strong customer reception to our newest surgical system."

Intuitive Surgical said that it ended 2025 with $9.03 billion in cash on hand.

Is ISRG Stock a Buy?

Intuitive Surgical's stock has a consensus Strong Buy rating among 14 Wall Street analysts. That rating is based on 11 Buy and three Hold recommendations issued in the last three months. The average ISRG price target of $645.08 implies 22.68% upside from current levels. These ratings could change after the company's financial results.
data.author: Joel Baglole

data.title: COF Earnings: Capital One Posts Mixed Results and Announces Acquisition of Startup 'Brex'
data.description:
Credit card issuer Capital One (COF) has reported mixed financial results for the fourth quarter of 2025, and announced that it is paying $5.15 billion to acquire startup company Brex.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

McLean, Virginia-based Capital One Financial Corp. announced earnings per share (EPS) of $3.86, which fell short of the $4.12 expected on Wall Street. Revenue in the quarter totaled $15.58 billion, which exceeded the $15.37 billion consensus forecast of analysts.

For its entire fiscal year, Capital One reported a profit of $2.45 billion, or $4.03 per share. Revenue was reported as $53.43 billion. However, the latest print from the credit card issuer was overshadowed by news that it is embarking on a new acquisition. Capital One recently completed its $35.30 billion all-stock purchase of former rival Discover Financial Services.

Capital One's Brex Acquisition

Capital One said that it is acquiring startup Brex for $5.15 billion, the latest deal undertaken by the credit card giant's CEO Richard Fairbank. Capital One said it will pay 50% cash and 50% stock for Brex, which is an artificial intelligence-powered financial technology platform that provides corporate credit cards, expense management, and business accounts for other startup companies.

Shares of the bank fell about 4%.

Under Fairbank, a rare founder-CEO of a major U.S. bank, Capital One acquired rival card firm Discover Financial last year for about $35 billion. That deal was Fairbank's crowning achievement, giving the credit card lender access to one of the only payment networks of any scale.

"Since our founding, we set out to build a payments company at the frontier of the technology revolution," Fairbank said in a release. "Acquiring Brex accelerates this journey, especially in the business payments marketplace."

Fairbank said that Brex pioneered the combination of corporate cards, banking and spend management software: "They have taken the rarest of journeys for a fintech, building a vertically integrated platform from the bottom of the tech stack to the top," he said.
data.author: Joel Baglole

data.title: Stock Market Today: SPY, QQQ Rally on Trump's Greenland U-Turn as Inflation Inches Higher
data.description:
Both the S&P 500 ETF (SPY) and the Nasdaq 100 ETF (QQQ) closed higher on Thursday as investors were able to breathe a sigh of relief from President Trump decision to withdraw new tariffs on eight European countries. The reversal came after he announced a framework deal to acquire land in Greenland.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The deal to acquire the self-governing territory is still in the works. However, it reportedly includes terms to provide the U.S. with mineral rights and ownership of small pockets of land for military use while allowing Denmark to remain in control.

The removal of the tariffs has put the U.S.-EU trade deal back on track, with a ratification vote on the agreement coming as soon as this month. The deal exempts most U.S. products from tariffs while placing a 15% tariff on most EU exports and a 50% duty on steel and aluminum.

Meanwhile, the Bureau of Economic Analysis' (BEA) second estimate of third-quarter gross domestic product (GDP) showed an annualized growth rate of 4.4%, higher than the expectation of 4.3% and reaching the highest level since the third quarter of 2023. The BEA's first estimate showed 4.3% growth. The upward revision was driven by a 3.5% increase in consumer spending, which accounts for about two-thirds of GDP. Higher investments, exports, and government spending also contributed to the growth.

In another report, the BEA said that consumer spending grew by 0.5% month-over-month in both October and November. Purchases of financial services, healthcare, insurance, gas, and other energy items contributed to the growth.

The economic data didn't stop there, with November's personal consumption expenditures (PCE) index increasing by 2.8%, matching the consensus estimate and rising from 2.7% in October. Core PCE, which excludes volatile food and energy items, was 2.8% and in line with the estimate as well. The PCE index tracks changes in the prices paid by consumers for goods and services, while core PCE is the Fed's preferred gauge of inflation.

Finally, Trump warned that Europe would face "big retaliation" if it decides to dump U.S. assets. Between April and November 2025, European investors accounted for the majority of U.S. Treasury purchases, making up roughly 80% of all foreign buying, according to Citi (C). In addition, Europe holds nearly 40% of all U.S. Treasuries owned by foreign investors.

The S&P 500 (SPX) closed with a 0.52% gain, while the Nasdaq 100 (NDX) returned 0.76%.

Stay ahead of macro events with our up-to-the-minute Economic Calendar — filter by impact, country, and more.
data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: McCormick Stock (MKC) Closes 8% Lower as Earnings Disappoint
data.description:
The stock of McCormick & Co. (MKC) closed 8% lower on Jan. 22 after the spice maker reported disappointing financial results and offered mixed guidance for 2026.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

McCormick announced earnings per share (EPS) of $0.86 for what was its fiscal fourth quarter. That was below analysts' consensus estimates of $0.88. Revenue totaled $1.85 billion, up 3% from a year earlier but only slightly beating the $1.84 billion forecast on Wall Street.

MKC stock closed at its lowest level since Oct. 24, 2023. It was the second-worst performer in the S&P 500 index on the day. As for guidance, management said that they expect revenue growth to accelerate to between 13% and 17% this year. However, it forecast earnings growth of just 2% to 5%, which was below analysts' expectations.

Tough Times for the Spice Business

Management at McCormick, which specializes in food spices, blamed the poor print and weak outlook on consumers who are pulling back their discretionary spending and looking for alternatives when it comes to groceries and lowering their food bills.

"While global trade dynamics continue to create headwinds and we are facing elevated costs for the year, we are leveraging our competitive advantages, productivity initiatives, and cost management discipline to help mitigate these pressures, sustain volume growth, and fund investments that drive long-term value creation," said CEO Brendan Foley in the company's earnings release.

Is MKC Stock a Buy?

McCormick stock has a consensus Strong Buy rating among five Wall Street analysts. That rating is based on four Buy and one Hold recommendations issued in the last three months. The average MKC price target of $80.40 implies 31.37% upside from current levels. These ratings could change after the company's financial results.
data.author: Joel Baglole

data.title: "We Couldn't Find a Way to Compete…" Ford Stock (NYSE:F) Slips as Sedans May Make a Comeback
data.description:
One odd departure for legacy automaker Ford (F) is that it really no longer sells cars. At least, not in the conventional sense; it sells vehicles, yes, but most of these are trucks or SUVs rather than cars in the truest sense. But Ford may be making a comeback to cars, or rather sedans, as a growing call for affordability emerges. This news did not sit well with shareholders, as shares slipped fractionally in Thursday afternoon's trading.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Recently, during the Detroit Auto Show, Ford CEO Jim Farley was asked if there were plans to go back to building cars, cars like the Taurus, the Fusion, or the Gran Torino. Farley did not rule the idea out immediately, instead calling on the audience to "never say never."

Farley also revealed a telling point, saying, "The sedan market is very vibrant. It's not that there isn't a market there. It's just we couldn't find a way to compete and be profitable. Well, we may find a way to do that." There is a value in a sedan, particularly for single people, and those looking for their first car, which for many was often an older sedan. The idea that the sedan could be brought back successfully to help bridge the mounting affordability gap is a point that needs to be closely considered.

The Real Top-Selling Truck Brand

As it turns out, determining the top-selling truck brand in the United States is a bit of a challenge, and it all really depends on a few key definitions. One report recently analyzed the numbers, and noted that both Ford and General Motors (GM) had a right to claim the term.

As it turns out, Ford can make a case, noting that its F-Series lineup sold 828,832 trucks in 2025. This easily swamps the GMC Sierra, which sold 356,218 trucks, and the Chevrolet Silverado, which sold 588,709 trucks. But lest you reach for your calculator, indeed, GM sold a combined total of 944,927 trucks, which blasts the F-Series out of the water. So determining who the biggest seller is is a matter of determining if you mean by brand, in which GM wins, or by truck line, in which Ford wins.

Is Ford Stock a Good Buy Right Now?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Hold consensus rating on F stock based on three Buys, 11 Holds and one Sell assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. After a 35.53% rally in its share price over the past year, the average F price target of $13.84 per share implies 0.73% upside potential.
data.author: Steve Anderson

data.title: BitGo's $212.8M IPO Puts Crypto Stocks Back on Investors' Radar
data.description:
Crypto stocks may be edging back into the spotlight. U.S.-based BitGo priced its IPO above expectations on Wednesday, raising $212.8 million. The offering marks the first public listing by a digital asset company in 2026, despite ongoing regulatory hurdles and volatility in the crypto market. BitGo's debut serves as an early test of investor demand. While the IPO signals renewed interest in crypto stocks, a full comeback will likely require steadier markets and clearer regulations.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

For context, BitGo is a crypto custody and security firm that helps institutions store, manage, and protect digital assets. It provides regulated infrastructure for trading, staking, and settlement across the crypto market.

More Details on BitGo IPO

BitGo's shares will trade on the New York Stock Exchange (NYSE) on Thursday under the ticker BTGO. The company sold 11.8 million shares at $18 each, topping its earlier price range of $15 to $17 per share.

Overall, the offering values BitGo at about $2.08 billion.

Tough Times for Crypto Stocks

Interestingly, the IPO comes at a challenging time for the U.S. crypto industry. Lawmakers are moving forward with a long-awaited market structure bill that could reshape how crypto is regulated.

Additionally, the sector has also been rattled by a sharp crypto selloff in October, making investors more cautious and raising the bar for companies trying to raise money. Reports say crypto exchange Kraken is among the firms considering IPOs this year.

BitGo is the first crypto company to move ahead with a U.S. IPO in 2026, after several digital asset firms went public last year. For instance, Circle (CRCL) and Figure (FIGR) went public earlier in 2025 during a much stronger period for crypto stocks. That more upbeat market helped both companies post sharp gains on their first day of trading.

data.author: Kirti Tak
